address: Wat That subdistrict, Mueang Nong Khai, Nong Khai, Thailand
opening_hours: The provincial e-book lists daytime daily access; recheck exact current hours locally before you go.

description.en: Nong Khai's most distinctive cultural site, built around a large sculpture park of Buddhist, Brahmanical, and local symbolic imagery.
description.nl: De meest onderscheidende culturele plek van Nong Khai, een groot beeldenpark vol boeddhistische, brahmaanse en lokale symboliek.

metaDescription.en: Why Sala Kaeo Ku is Nong Khai's most distinctive cultural attraction and how to visit it as more than a quick photo stop.
metaDescription.nl: Waarom Sala Kaeo Ku de meest onderscheidende culturele attractie van Nong Khai is en hoe je het bezoekt als meer dan een snelle fotostop.

enhanced_description:
Sala Kaeo Ku is the Nong Khai stop that feels genuinely unlike anywhere else in the city's core cluster. The sculpture park matters because it turns overlapping Buddhist, Brahmanical, and local symbolic worlds into a place you can physically walk through, rather than reducing Nong Khai to markets and border infrastructure alone.

That is why it deserves more than a novelty label. When you give it time, the site starts to read less like a quirky sculpture garden and more like a deliberate worldview built in concrete and mortar.
detailed_history: The provincial Nong Khai e-book still links Sala Kaeo Ku to Luang Pu Bunleua and describes the park as a place where different religions and symbolic traditions were intentionally placed together through 208 statues and written explanations.
visitor_experience: Expect an outdoor sculpture park that rewards slower walking and close attention. The visit is stronger when you give the statues enough time to register as a whole narrative environment rather than racing only toward the biggest photo angles.

best_time_to_visit.crowd_level: Usually manageable outside peak midday heat

practical_info.how_to_get_there: Reached by short drive or local transport from central Nong Khai.
practical_info.nearby_attractions: Best paired with the city core rather than with a long same-day Sangkhom run.
practical_info.food_options: Plan your main meal back in the city unless you are already building a broader outer-city route.
practical_info.shopping: Use the city core and Tha Sadet for shopping instead of expecting that from the park itself.

insider_tips: Do not rush this if you want the site to feel coherent.
This is better as a daytime cultural stop than as a sunset add-on.
Recheck current fees locally instead of relying on older static numbers.
verified_note: Source-backed summary based on the provincial Nong Khai e-book. Current local ticketing and hours should still be rechecked before visiting.
cultural_significance: Sala Kaeo Ku remains one of the clearest places in Nong Khai to see how local religious imagination and symbolic traditions have been expressed in built form.
